## API Endpoint Comparison: Documentation vs Implementation

| Endpoint | Method | Status |
|----------|--------|--------|
| `/api/users` | GET | ✅ **Documented & Implemented** |
| `/api/users` | POST | ✅ **Documented & Implemented** |
| `/api/health` | GET | ✅ **Documented & Implemented** |

### Summary

All three endpoints documented in `projects/webapp/docs/README.md` are implemented in the source code:

1. **`GET /api/users`** — Listed in README and implemented in `UsersController.scala` (the `handle` method returns a JSON array of users)

2. **`POST /api/users`** — Listed in README and implemented in `UsersController.scala` (the `handle` method returns `{"status":"created"}` with status 201)

3. **`GET /api/health`** — Listed in README and implemented directly in `Main.scala` (returns `200 OK`)

There are no undocumented endpoints—the implementation matches the documentation exactly. Both the README and source code are aligned on the three endpoints offered by the webapp.
